consistency.casting_time_duration_fit: Consistent; Ritual spells often have longer casting times and durations.
consistency.components_match_effect: Consistent; Material component (lead-based ink) suggests permanent/long-lasting inscription.
consistency.class_suitability: Consistent; Illusion magic is standard for Bard, Warlock, and Wizard.

quality.description_clarity: High. Clearly defines the dual appearance of the writing and the conditions for reading it (True Sight, dispelling).
quality.mechanical_impact: Moderate. Offers utility for secrecy, misinformation, and secure communication over a long duration (10 days). The ritual tag enhances its utility for preparation.
quality.component_cost_justification: The 10 gp cost tied to the consumed ink is appropriate for a 1st-level spell with a 10-day duration, ensuring the illusion is robust.

mechanics.components: S, M (a lead-based ink worth at least 10 gp, which the spell consumes)
mechanics.effect_summary: Alters the appearance of written text on a surface so that only the caster and designated creatures see the true message, while others see gibberish or a false message. Dispelling removes both messages.
countermeasures: Truesight (allows reading of the hidden message)
